description: RGB shows Kelowna urban grid along Okanagan Lake with a bridge and mixed suburban/agricultural land; SWIR shows uniform healthy green vegetation throughout with no burn scar or thermal anomaly visible.
labeler_notes: Clean urban tile well past event window — no wildfire aftermath detectable in this footprint.

description: RGB shows a dark brown corridor running along the hillside terrain northeast of the lake, consistent with a burn scar; SWIR confirms with a distinct brownish-tan band of reduced vegetation reflectance flanked by healthier green zones.
labeler_notes: Burn scar signature is spatially coherent in both views — well-defined corridor matches post-wildfire aftermath.

description: RGB shows rugged dark forest/scrub terrain with a cloud band across the upper portion partially obscuring the scene; SWIR reveals mostly green healthy vegetation with some yellow-orange stressed patches in the lower right but no clear burn scar.
labeler_notes: Pre-event capture; cloud at top limits upper portion. Stressed patches in SWIR lower right are modest and insufficient to confirm hazard.

description: RGB is dominated by thick white cloud cover occupying roughly 80% of the frame with only narrow dark fringe at corners; SWIR similarly cloud-saturated with almost no ground signal.
labeler_notes: Insufficient ground visibility in both bands to make any assessment.

description: RGB shows a uniformly dark/hazy brown cast over dense terrain with a visible road network, consistent with heavy smoke overlay during or immediately after fire activity; SWIR penetrates haze to show stressed yellow-orange vegetation in the upper portion and mixed green below, confirming elevated vegetation stress across the scene.
labeler_notes: Smoke/haze in RGB substantially degrades optical clarity; SWIR smoke penetration reveals widespread vegetation stress matching peak-event timing.

description: RGB shows dry brownish-tan terrain with scattered green patches and urban areas — typical dry scrubland or light post-fire mix; SWIR shows predominantly yellow-green tones with orange-brown patches indicating stressed or dry vegetation but no dominant burn scar signature.
labeler_notes: Stress signal present in SWIR but not at the level or spatial pattern expected for a confirmed burn scar; scene reads as dry landscape or marginal stress.

description: RGB shows a large dark grey zone covering most of the frame with residual smoke visible at the lower-right corner and brown terrain at the top edge; SWIR confirms an extensive dark brown/black region across the lower two-thirds of the scene, a clear burn scar signature nine days after the event peak.
labeler_notes: Smoke and residual haze reduce RGB clarity but SWIR unambiguously shows large-scale burn scar — would escalate to CRITICAL if active fire glow were confirmed, but only scar and smoke are visible.

description: RGB shows hazy grey terrain with thin cloud/smoke streaks and some brown patchy areas; SWIR shows mixed yellow-green with a narrow brownish strip at the lower edge and some orange-yellow stressed zones but no widespread dark burn scar.
labeler_notes: Haze and thin cloud limit confidence; limited stressed vegetation visible in SWIR but no clear burn scar extent to confirm hazard.

description: RGB is dominated by thick white cloud cover filling the upper 70% of the frame with only a narrow dark strip at the bottom; SWIR similarly cloud-dominated with only a small brownish-orange fringe at the bottom that may indicate burn scar remnant.
labeler_notes: Cloud covers too much of the scene for reliable interpretation; the small ground strip at bottom is insufficient to assign a confident hazard label.

description: RGB shows urban area in the upper third with partial cloud patches across the lower scene and dark terrain patches visible through gaps; SWIR shows orange-yellow urban signature at top and brownish patches consistent with burn scar remnant visible through cloud gaps in the lower portion.
labeler_notes: Enough ground is visible through cloud gaps in SWIR to identify burn scar aftermath; cloud limits confidence but does not fully obstruct the scene.
